subtasks.title: Audit site structure and document conventions reference

artifact_quality: Reference document covers all 5 content collections (_posts, _blogs, _figs, _shots, _vlogs) with accurate front matter field listings
Reference includes exact naming conventions (YYYY-MM-DD-Slug.md), category naming patterns (hyphen-separated), and layout assignments
Reference documents the site navigation structure, sidebar links, and page hierarchy accurately
Reference captures the inline HTML title styling convention (e.g., <span style='color:#e32724'>) used in post titles
Reference includes image asset path conventions for each collection (public/fig_post/, public/shots/, etc.)
All information in the reference is verified against actual site files — no fabricated or assumed details
acceptance.verification: Cross-check at least 3 documented conventions against actual site files to confirm accuracy. Verify that front matter examples match real posts.

subtasks.order_reason: Must document existing conventions before validating integrity or creating templates, so all downstream tasks have an accurate reference.

subtasks.title: Validate site content integrity and build readiness

artifact_quality: All content files in _posts, _blogs, _figs, _shots, _vlogs have valid YAML front matter (no parse errors)
Every content file has required front matter fields: layout, title, date, categories
Layout values in front matter match actual layout files in _layouts/ (no orphaned references)
Jekyll build completes without fatal errors (warnings acceptable but documented)
Validation report lists any issues found with specific file paths and descriptions
acceptance.verification: Run a YAML front matter validation script across all content files. Attempt a Jekyll build (bundle exec jekyll build) and capture output. Report includes pass/fail per file.

subtasks.order_reason: Depends on 001 for the conventions reference to know what 'valid' means. Must validate before creating templates to ensure templates are based on working patterns.

subtasks.title: Create reusable content templates for each collection type

artifact_quality: One template file per collection type: post, blog, fig, shot, vlog — each with correct layout, front matter fields, and placeholder content
Templates use the exact front matter field names, ordering, and formatting found in existing site content
Each template includes inline comments or placeholders guiding the user on what to fill in (title, date, categories, image paths)
Templates follow the YYYY-MM-DD-Slug.md naming convention with a clear naming instruction
A quick-reference guide accompanies the templates summarizing when to use each collection type
acceptance.verification: Place a test post using one template into the appropriate collection folder and verify Jekyll builds without errors. Remove the test post after verification.

subtasks.order_reason: Depends on both 001 (conventions) and 002 (validated patterns) to ensure templates are based on correct, working conventions.
